supplementEntry.name: Fisetin
supplementEntry.evidenceTierRationale: Strong preclinical senolytic data (Yousefzadeh 2018 landmark paper), but human RCTs are small (n=10 to n=74). Largest completed RCT (knee OA, n=74) found no significant benefit. Multiple large trials ongoing but results not yet published.

cognitive: Neuroprotection via anti-inflammatory and antioxidant pathways
Potential neuroprotective adjunct in ischemic stroke

mechanismsOfAction: Selective elimination of senescent cells via PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway inhibition
Inhibition of pro-inflammatory cytokines (IL-8, TNF-alpha, IL-6)
Reduction of matrix metalloproteinases (MMP-2, MMP-7, MMP-9)
Activation of Nrf2 antioxidant response pathway
Reduction of SASP (senescence-associated secretory phenotype) factors
Free radical scavenging and ROS reduction

commonSideEffects: Mild GI upset (nausea, bloating, decreased appetite)
drugInteractions: Potential interaction with thrombolytics (enhanced rt-PA effects)
Possible additive effects with other senolytics (dasatinib + quercetin)

effectSizes.il8Reduction: Significant between-group difference (p<0.05) at 100 mg/day for 7 weeks in CRC patients (Farsad-Naeimi 2018, n=37)
effectSizes.strokeOutcome: Significantly lower NIHSS scores in delayed-OTT fisetin group vs placebo (Wang 2019, n=215)
effectSizes.biologicalAging: Mixed: 4/10 reduced, 5/10 increased, 1/10 no change (Lee 2024, n=10)
effectSizes.bioavailabilityEnhancement: Cmax 23.9x and AUC 26.9x improvement with hydrogel formulation vs unformulated (Krishnakumar 2022, n=15)

keyCitations.title: Effect of fisetin supplementation on inflammatory factors and matrix metalloproteinase enzymes in colorectal cancer patients
Fisetin Prolongs Therapy Window of Brain Ischemic Stroke Using Tissue Plasminogen Activator: A Double-Blind Randomized Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trial
Fisetin is a senotherapeutic that extends health and lifespan
The Effects of Fisetin on Reducing Biological Aging: A Pilot Study

mechanisms.mechanism: Selective elimination of senescent cells via PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway inhibition

evidence.title: Fisetin is a senotherapeutic that extends health and lifespan
evidence.findings: Fisetin was the most potent senolytic among 10 flavonoids tested. Reduced senescence markers in multiple tissues. Extended median and maximum lifespan in mice. Human adipose tissue confirmed selective senescent cell clearance.
evidence.methodology: Screening of 10 flavonoids in senescent human fibroblasts, murine lifespan studies, and human adipose tissue explant validation

mechanisms.mechanism: Inhibition of pro-inflammatory cytokines and NF-kB signaling

evidence.title: Effect of fisetin supplementation on inflammatory factors and matrix metalloproteinase enzymes in colorectal cancer patients
evidence.findings: Fisetin 100 mg/day for 7 weeks significantly reduced plasma IL-8 and hs-CRP levels. MMP-7 levels were suppressed. Between-group comparison showed statistically significant reduction for IL-8.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in 37 colorectal cancer patients (18 fisetin, 19 placebo)

evidence.title: Fisetin Prolongs Therapy Window of Brain Ischemic Stroke Using Tissue Plasminogen Activator: A Double-Blind Randomized Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trial
evidence.findings: Fisetin 100 mg/day combined with rt-PA improved treatment outcomes in delayed onset-to-treatment-time strata, with lower NIHSS scores. Reduced serum MMP-2, MMP-9, and CRP levels.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in 215 acute ischemic stroke patients

benefits.specificClaim: Reduces systemic inflammatory markers in cancer patients

evidence.title: Effect of fisetin supplementation on inflammatory factors and matrix metalloproteinase enzymes in colorectal cancer patients
evidence.findings: Fisetin 100 mg/day significantly reduced plasma IL-8 (significant between-group, p<0.05) and hs-CRP levels. MMP-7 suppressed within the fisetin group.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in colorectal cancer patients

benefits.specificClaim: Extends therapeutic window for rt-PA in ischemic stroke

evidence.title: Fisetin Prolongs Therapy Window of Brain Ischemic Stroke Using Tissue Plasminogen Activator: A Double-Blind Randomized Placebo-Controlled Clinical Trial
evidence.findings: Fisetin 100 mg/day combined with rt-PA dramatically improved treatment outcomes in delayed onset-to-treatment-time strata. Lower NIHSS scores and reduced serum MMP-2, MMP-9, CRP.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind RCT in acute ischemic stroke patients

benefits.specificClaim: Senolytic clearance of senescent cells for healthspan extension

evidence.title: The Effects of Fisetin on Reducing Biological Aging: A Pilot Study
evidence.journal: Alternative Therapies in Health and Medicine
evidence.findings: Fisetin 500 mg daily for 1 week/month for 6 months. Mixed results: 4/10 reduced biological age, 5/10 increased, 1/10 no change. No adverse effects. Telomere lengths showed no significant changes.
evidence.methodology: Open-label pilot study in healthy adults over age 50

safety.claim: Good safety profile across multiple completed RCTs with doses up to 20 mg/kg

evidence.title: Results from a Randomized Clinical Trial Evaluating the Senolytic Fisetin for Treating Knee Osteoarthritis
evidence.findings: 3 dosing cycles of fisetin 20 mg/kg for 2 consecutive days with 28-day breaks. No serious safety concerns. 185 AEs reported (minor: joint pain, nausea, headache, dry mouth), no significant between-group differences in AEs.
evidence.methodology: Double-blind, placebo-controlled Phase I/II RCT in 74 knee OA patients (age 40-80)

evidence.title: Fisetin for COVID-19 in skilled nursing facilities: Senolytic trials in the COVID era
evidence.journal: Journal of the American Geriatrics Society
evidence.findings: NIH-funded trial design testing fisetin in SARS-CoV-2 positive nursing facility residents. Addressed logistical challenges of senolytic trials in long-term care during pandemic.
evidence.methodology: Multicenter placebo-controlled clinical trial design paper

safety.claim: Extremely poor oral bioavailability of unformulated fisetin requiring enhanced delivery systems

evidence.title: Enhanced bioavailability and pharmacokinetics of a novel hybrid-hydrogel formulation of fisetin orally administered in healthy individuals: a randomised double-blinded comparative crossover study
evidence.findings: First human PK study. Unformulated fisetin (1000 mg) had very poor bioavailability -- detectable in plasma for only ~2 hours. Novel hydrogel formulation (192 mg fisetin) achieved 23.9-fold Cmax and 26.9-fold AUC enhancement.
evidence.methodology: Randomized double-blind crossover PK study in 15 healthy volunteers

dosage.dosageRange: 100-500 mg/day (continuous) or 20 mg/kg x 2 days (intermittent senolytic)
dosage.claim: Dose range established across multiple clinical protocols

evidence.title: Fisetin as a senotherapeutic agent: Evidence and perspectives for age-related diseases
evidence.findings: Synthesized evidence across clinical trials. Anti-inflammatory doses: 100 mg/day continuous. Senolytic doses: 20 mg/kg/day for 2 consecutive days in intermittent cycles. Emphasized need for more research on optimal dosing.
evidence.methodology: Comprehensive review of in vitro, animal, and Phase I/II clinical trial evidence

evidence.title: Senolytics To slOw Progression of Sepsis (STOP-Sepsis) in elderly patients: Study protocol for a multicenter, randomized, adaptive allocation clinical trial
evidence.findings: Protocol testing fisetin 20 mg/kg single dose vs two doses (1 day apart) vs placebo in 220 planned sepsis patients. Primary outcome: composite cardiovascular/respiratory/renal SOFA scores at 7 days.
evidence.methodology: Multicenter, randomized, double-blind, adaptive allocation Phase 2 trial protocol
